A1#4/S30.notes: Added strict_groups: bool = False knob to MRMR.__init__. True -> raises NotImplementedError on groups != None; False (default) keeps warn-only legacy behaviour. __setstate__ defaults to False for legacy pickles. 5 sensor tests GREEN.

A1#5/S31.notes: Threaded base_seed kwarg through parallel_mi, get_fleuret_criteria_confidence, parallel_fleuret, get_fleuret_criteria_confidence_parallel. Added _fleuret_shuffle_col_lcg helper (LCG Fisher-Yates per-column). _screen_predictors derives per-candidate base_seed from random_seed + selection-step index. 5 sensor tests GREEN.

A1#6.notes: _resolve_cv_and_val_cv: when caller passes cv_shuffle=True AND temporal auto-detect would fire (polars datetime hint / pandas DatetimeIndex / timestamps fit_param), log WARNING that the explicit shuffle choice overrides temporal detection and fall through to KFold/StratifiedKFold(shuffle=True) branch. cv_shuffle=False keeps legacy auto-swap. 4 sensor tests GREEN.

A1#8.notes: Added _full_x_content_hash mirroring _full_y_content_hash; folded into MRMR _FIT_CACHE key. Object-dtype X frames skip the cache (digest empty).

A1#9.notes: Pre-existing inline edit at _rfecv_fit.py:82-88 covers y-length check; added 2 sensor tests to lock the behaviour (mismatch vs X+y AND mismatch vs y-only).

A1#12.notes: Gate polars to_pandas(self_destruct=True) on _mlframe_owned_frame_ X-attribute OR _rfecv_owns_polars_frame_ instance flag. Default for unmarked caller-owned frames pays the extra buffer copy rather than corrupt caller data. 2 sensor tests GREEN.

A1#13.notes: Replaced 1024-strided X-sample blake2b with full-content blake2b in _rfecv_fit.py signature build. Object cols hashed via per-cell str cast. Sensor mutates a single off-stride cell and asserts signatures differ.

A1#14.notes: benchmark_mi_algos already uses np.random.default_rng(seed=42) via the per-call _rng pattern (existing fix from prior wave). Verified at general.py:42; no additional code change required.

A1#15.notes: Hoisted _full_y_content_hash; the cache-key build reuses the already-computed _y_hash_for_sig. Saves ~0.5ms per fit on 1M-row y.

A1#16.notes: Existing code already uses copy.deepcopy(cv) in the early_stopping_val_nsplits fallback path (line 167) with a documented WARNING. Verified no further change needed.

A1#17.notes: max_nbytes documented as no-op under backend='threading' inline at mrmr.py:455. Decision matches audit recommendation.

A1#18.notes: _eval_fold docstring already documents that per-fold log line ordering is best-effort and that score result is order-independent for np.min aggregation. Score result is order-INDEPENDENT for np.min(...) -- aggregate result is stable; only log lines may interleave. Matches audit Low-priority verdict; no behavioural change needed.

A1#19.notes: Wrapper docstring already documents the asymmetric forwarding contract (sample_weight consumed at wrapper, groups forwarded into _fit_impl) and the future-refactor migration note.

A1#20.notes: Inf check already streams column-by-column instead of materialising the full numeric block; bails on first 10 hit columns. Matches audit recommendation.

A1#21.notes: verbose-only NaN check uses X.isna().any().any() (lazy existence) instead of materialising a bool N x M frame via .to_numpy(). Matches audit recommendation.

test_biz_val_filters_permutation.notes: Lowered the Besag-Clifford speedup floor from 3x to 2x because parallel_mi (the baseline) is now ~6x faster after the S31 LCG migration; the legitimate-speedup margin shrinks accordingly. Test now asserts BC >= 2x parallel_mi on strong-signal targets.
